name: Autodesk Fusion API Vocabulary
description: Key domain terms and concepts from Autodesk Fusion and Autodesk Platform Services (APS) APIs.

terms.label: Autodesk Platform Services
terms.definition: The developer platform formerly known as Forge that underpins Autodesk cloud APIs including authentication, data management, model derivative, OSS, webhooks, and Fusion-specific services.

terms.label: Hub
terms.definition: A top-level container in APS Data Management representing an Autodesk account or BIM 360/ACC account grouping projects.

terms.label: Project
terms.definition: A collaborative workspace within a Hub containing folders, items, and versions of design files.

terms.label: Item
terms.definition: A persistent design file entity within a project folder that can have multiple versions.

terms.label: Version
terms.definition: A specific revision of an Item, referencing a storage object in OSS.

terms.label: Object Storage Service
terms.definition: APS service for storing and retrieving raw binary files (PDFs, DWGs, RVTs, etc.) organised in buckets.

terms.label: Bucket
terms.definition: A named OSS container that holds uploaded objects. Buckets can have transient, temporary, or persistent retention policies.

terms.label: Model Derivative
terms.definition: APS service that translates source CAD files into viewer-ready SVF2, glTF, STL, and other derivative formats and extracts metadata.

terms.label: Derivative Manifest
terms.definition: A JSON document returned by the Model Derivative API describing all derivatives generated for a source design, including their URNs and processing status.

terms.label: Uniform Resource Name
terms.definition: A base64-encoded identifier used in APS to reference stored objects, derivatives, and design versions.

terms.label: Webhook
terms.definition: An HTTP callback registered via the APS Webhooks API that delivers event notifications for data management, derivative processing, and other APS events.

terms.label: Design Automation
terms.definition: APS service that runs Fusion (and AutoCAD/Revit/Inventor) automation tasks in the cloud without desktop software, using Activities and WorkItems.

terms.label: Activity
terms.definition: A Design Automation definition specifying the engine, app bundle, script, and input/output parameters for an automation job.

terms.label: WorkItem
terms.definition: A Design Automation job instance that executes an Activity with specific input files and produces output files.

terms.label: Flex Tokens
terms.definition: Autodesk's pay-per-use consumption credit used to meter premium APS services such as Fusion Automation API (3.0 tokens per processing hour).

terms.label: Manufacturing Data Model API
terms.definition: GraphQL API providing cloud-based read and write access to Fusion component properties, Bill of Materials, model hierarchy, and thumbnail assets.

terms.label: Bill of Materials
terms.definition: A structured list of components, sub-assemblies, and raw materials that make up a Fusion design, accessible via the Manufacturing Data Model GraphQL API.

terms.label: Fusion Operations API
terms.definition: REST API (V2 Public Beta) for integrating Autodesk Fusion Operations MES data — production orders, inventory, machine status, downtime, punch-clock, and workers — with ERP and BI systems.

terms.label: OAuth 2.0
terms.definition: The authentication standard used by all APS APIs, supporting both 2-legged (client credentials) and 3-legged (authorization code) flows.

terms.label: 3-Legged OAuth Token
terms.definition: An APS access token obtained via authorization code flow on behalf of an end user, required for accessing user-owned data in Data Management and Fusion APIs.

terms.label: 2-Legged OAuth Token
terms.definition: An APS access token obtained via client credentials flow for server-to-server calls not requiring end-user consent.

terms.label: Secure Service Account
terms.definition: APS service for managing service-account credentials and short-lived exchange tokens for server-to-server integrations.

terms.label: Fusion Add-in
terms.definition: A plugin written in Python or C++ that extends the Fusion desktop application by adding commands, panels, and automated workflows using the Fusion API.
